title: ECG Authentication Neural Network Hardware Design with Collective Optimization of Low Precision and Structured Compression

method.input_representation: 160 ECG samples aligned at the R-peak, normalized by mean and standard deviation
method.training_strategy: Authentication-specific cost function maximizing inter-individual distance and minimizing intra-individual distance; joint optimization of Coarse-grain sparsity (CGS) and low-precision quantization

evaluation.context: Achieved for an in-house 741-subject database using 2-bit weight precision and 8X CGS compression

evaluation_protocol: Split into training (18,306 beats) and testing (52,849 beats) sets; used cosine similarity between registered and identification feature vectors

key_contribution: Design of a low-power ECG authentication ASIC
Implementation of a compressed DNN with 2-bit weights and structured sparsity to minimize memory and energy
Use of an authentication-specific cost function to optimize EER
main_strength: Significant memory reduction (104X compared to 32-bit floating point DNN)
Scalability to a large subject database (741 subjects)
main_weakness: No empirical testing of presentation attacks (spoofing)
insights.notes_for_review: The paper focuses heavily on the hardware optimization (area, power, memory) of the DNN rather than the biometric signal's stability over time or in various environments.
